crops.season_note: Prices typically fall right after harvest (Sept-Oct) and rise in the lean season (May-July). Selling in the lean season usually gets a better price if storage is available.
Cassava does not store well once uprooted; prices are fairly stable year-round, so sell soon after harvest rather than holding.
Highly seasonal — prices spike in the dry season (Dec-Mar) when supply is low, and crash in the rainy season glut (Jun-Aug). Perishable, so timing sale around demand spikes matters more than holding.
Stores well when dried properly. Prices tend to rise steadily from harvest (Oct) toward the following planting season (Apr-May), so holding in a dry, pest-free store can pay off.
